nouns.places.outside: Outside is anywhere out in the open air, away from indoors.
You go outside to play, run, and feel the sun or the wind.
Try it like this: 'I want to go outside!'
nouns.places.park: A park is a big open place with grass and things to play on.
You can swing, slide, run, and have a picnic at the park.
Try it like this: 'Let's go to the park.'
nouns.places.school: School is the place where kids go to learn and play with friends.
Teachers help you read, count, make art, and have recess.
Try it like this: 'I go to school.'
nouns.places.home: Home is the place where you live and feel safe.
It's where your bed, your toys, and your family are.
Try it like this: 'I want to go home.'
nouns.places.store: A store is a place where you buy food and things you need.
You push a cart, pick things off the shelves, and pay at the end.
Try it like this: 'We're going to the store.'
nouns.school.pencil: A pencil is a thin wooden stick you write and draw with.
It has a pointy gray tip, and an eraser to fix mistakes.
Try it like this: 'I need a pencil.'
nouns.school.paper: Paper is a thin white sheet you write, draw, and color on.
You can fold it, cut it, or hang your picture on the wall.
Try it like this: 'More paper, please.'
nouns.school.marker: A marker is a fat pen with bright, bold colors.
You pull off the cap and color — the lines come out thick.
Try it like this: 'I want the red marker.'
nouns.school.scissors: Scissors are a tool with two blades that cut paper.
You put your fingers in the holes and open and close to snip.
Try it like this: 'Help me with the scissors.'
nouns.school.glue: Glue is sticky stuff that holds two things together.
You spread a little, press the pieces, and let it dry.
Try it like this: 'I need glue.'
nouns.school.backpack: A backpack is a bag you wear on your back.
It holds your books, lunch, and things you take to school.
Try it like this: 'Where's my backpack?'
nouns.school.desk: A desk is a table where you sit to do your work.
It has a flat top to write on and a spot for your things.
Try it like this: 'I sit at my desk.'
nouns.school.classroom: A classroom is the room at school where your class learns.
It has desks, a board, books, and your teacher.
Try it like this: 'We go back to the classroom.'
nouns.school.recess: Recess is the time at school when you get to go play.
You run, climb, and play with friends outside.
Try it like this: 'I love recess!'
nouns.school.lunch: Lunch is the meal you eat in the middle of the day.
You sit with friends and eat the food you brought or got.
Try it like this: 'It's time for lunch.'
nouns.school.more.whiteboard: A whiteboard is a big smooth white board on the wall.
The teacher writes on it with markers everyone can see.
Try it like this: 'Look at the whiteboard.'
nouns.school.more.eraser: An eraser is a soft tool that rubs out pencil marks.
You rub it back and forth to make a mistake disappear.
Try it like this: 'I need the eraser.'
nouns.school.more.notebook: A notebook is a stack of paper held together to write in.
You flip the pages and keep all your work in one place.
Try it like this: 'Open your notebook.'
nouns.school.more.ruler: A ruler is a straight tool that helps you measure and draw lines.
It has little marks and numbers along the edge.
Try it like this: 'Use the ruler.'
nouns.school.more.bookshelf: A bookshelf is a tall set of shelves that holds books.
You slide a book out to read it and slide it back after.
Try it like this: 'Put it on the bookshelf.'
needs.holidays.birthday: Your birthday is the special day you were born, once a year.
There's cake, candles, presents, and singing just for you.
Try it like this: 'It's my birthday!'
needs.holidays.christmas: Christmas is a winter holiday many families celebrate.
People put up a tree, give presents, and gather together.
needs.holidays.hanukkah: Hanukkah is a Jewish holiday in winter that lasts eight nights.
Families light a candle each night on a special menorah.
needs.holidays.halloween: Halloween is a fall night when kids dress up in costumes.
You go door to door saying 'trick or treat' to get candy.
needs.holidays.thanksgiving: Thanksgiving is a fall holiday about being thankful.
Families share a big meal and say what they're glad for.
needs.holidays.easter: Easter is a spring holiday many families celebrate.
Kids hunt for colorful eggs that are hidden around.
needs.holidays.valentines: Valentine's Day is a day to show people you love them.
You give cards and hearts to friends and family.
Try it like this: 'Happy Valentine's Day!'
needs.holidays.fourth_of_july: The Fourth of July is a summer holiday for the United States.
People watch fireworks light up the night sky.
Try it like this: 'Happy Fourth of July!'
needs.holidays.new_year: New Year is when one year ends and a fresh one begins.
People count down and cheer when the clock hits midnight.
Try it like this: 'Happy New Year!'
needs.holidays.mothers_day: Mother's Day is a day to thank and celebrate moms.
You give your mom a card, a hug, or help around the house.
Try it like this: 'Happy Mother's Day, Mom!'
needs.holidays.fathers_day: Father's Day is a day to thank and celebrate dads.
You give your dad a card, a hug, or spend the day together.
Try it like this: 'Happy Father's Day, Dad!'
personal.school.fire_drill: A fire drill is practice for getting out of the building safely.
A bell rings loudly and everyone walks outside in a line.
Try it like this: 'It's just a fire drill.'
personal.school.lockdown_drill: A lockdown drill is practice for staying safe and quiet inside.
The class moves away from the door and keeps very still.
Try it like this: 'It's just a drill, we're safe.'
personal.school.field_trip: A field trip is when your class travels somewhere fun to learn.
You ride a bus to a place like a zoo or a museum.
Try it like this: 'We have a field trip!'
personal.school.library_day: Library day is when your class visits the library.
You pick out a book to borrow and bring home to read.
Try it like this: 'Today is library day.'
personal.school.picture_day: Picture day is when someone takes your photo at school.
You smile for the camera, and the photo goes home to your family.
Try it like this: 'It's picture day!'
personal.school.show_and_tell: Show and tell is when you bring something special to share.
You hold it up and tell the class all about it.
Try it like this: 'I have show and tell.'
personal.school.specials_pe: PE is the special class where you move and play games.
You run, stretch, and play with balls in the gym.
Try it like this: 'We have PE today.'
personal.school.specials_art: Art is the special class where you make things and create.
You paint, draw, cut, and glue to make something of your own.
Try it like this: 'I love art class.'
personal.school.specials_music: Music is the special class where you sing and play sounds.
You sing songs, clap rhythms, and play instruments.
Try it like this: 'We have music today.'
personal.school.lunchroom: The lunchroom is the big room where everyone eats lunch.
You sit at long tables with kids from your school.
Try it like this: 'We go to the lunchroom.'
personal.school.arrival: Arrival is the start of the school day, when you get there.
You hang up your backpack and say good morning.
Try it like this: 'I just got to school.'
personal.school.dismissal: Dismissal is the end of the school day, when you go home.
You grab your backpack and head to the bus or car.
Try it like this: 'It's time to go home.'
personal.school.centers: Centers are different play and learning spots around the room.
You move from one to the next — blocks, books, art, and more.
Try it like this: 'It's center time.'
personal.school.line_up: Lining up means standing one behind the other in a row.
You wait in line so the class can walk somewhere together.
Try it like this: 'Time to line up.'
personal.school.quiet_time: Quiet time is a calm part of the day with very little noise.
You rest, look at a book, or take a little break.
Try it like this: 'It's quiet time now.'
personal.school.calm_corner: The calm corner is a cozy spot to go when you need to settle.
You can take deep breaths and feel better there.
Try it like this: 'I need the calm corner.'
personal.school.my_teacher: Your teacher is the grown-up who helps your class learn.
They read with you, keep you safe, and cheer you on.
Try it like this: 'That's my teacher.'
personal.school.my_para: Your aide is a grown-up who helps you through the day.
They stay close by to help with work and tricky moments.
Try it like this: 'My aide helps me.'
personal.school.class_pet: A class pet is an animal that lives in your classroom.
The whole class takes turns feeding it and caring for it.
Try it like this: 'I want to see the class pet.'
personal.school.class_job: A class job is a little task that is yours to do.
You might be the line leader or hand out the papers.
Try it like this: 'I have a class job.'
nouns.places.outdoor.sandbox: A sandbox is a box filled with sand to dig and build in.
You scoop, pour, and make sandcastles with your hands or a shovel.
Try it like this: 'I want to play in the sandbox.'
nouns.places.outdoor.swing: A swing is a seat that hangs and goes back and forth.
You pump your legs to fly up high, then come back down.
Try it like this: 'Push me on the swing!'
nouns.places.outdoor.slide: A slide is a smooth ramp you go down at the playground.
You climb up the ladder and zip all the way to the bottom.
Try it like this: 'I want to go down the slide.'
nouns.places.outdoor.sprinkler: A sprinkler sprays water all around on a hot day.
You run and jump through the cool spray to get wet and giggle.
Try it like this: 'Let's run in the sprinkler!'
nouns.places.outdoor.hose: A hose is a long tube that carries water outside.
You point it to water the grass or spray and play.
Try it like this: 'Turn on the hose.'
nouns.places.outdoor.sidewalk: A sidewalk is the hard path beside the road for walking.
You walk, ride a scooter, or draw with chalk on it.
Try it like this: 'Walk on the sidewalk.'
nouns.places.outdoor.driveway: A driveway is the spot where a car parks by a house.
It connects the street to the garage, and you can play on it.
Try it like this: 'The car is in the driveway.'
nouns.places.outdoor.mailbox: A mailbox is a box where letters and mail are dropped off.
The mail carrier puts in cards, letters, and packages.
Try it like this: 'Let's check the mailbox.'
nouns.places.geo.country: A country is a very big area of land with its own name and flag.
The United States is a country; so are Mexico and Japan.
Try it like this: 'What country is that?'
nouns.places.geo.city: A city is a big, busy place with lots of buildings and people.
It has tall buildings, busy streets, and many cars.
Try it like this: 'We live near a big city.'
nouns.places.geo.town: A town is a place where people live, smaller than a city.
It has houses, shops, and streets, but it's quieter.
Try it like this: 'We drove through town.'
nouns.places.geo.street: A street is a road where cars drive and houses line the sides.
You always look both ways before you cross a street.
Try it like this: 'Hold my hand by the street.'
nouns.places.geo.map: A map is a flat picture that shows where places are.
You follow the lines and dots to find your way.
Try it like this: 'Look at the map.'
nouns.places.geo.flag: A flag is a piece of cloth with colors and shapes that stands for a place.
It waves in the wind on a tall pole.
Try it like this: 'I see the flag.'
nouns.places.geo.ocean: The ocean is a huge body of salty water that goes on and on.
It has waves and fish, and is so big you can't see the far side.
Try it like this: 'I want to see the ocean.'
nouns.places.geo.mountain: A mountain is a giant hill of rock that rises way up high.
The very top can be so tall it has snow on it.
Try it like this: 'Look at the big mountain.'
nouns.places.geo.river: A river is a long line of water that flows across the land.
The water moves along, and boats and fish travel in it.
Try it like this: 'There's a river.'
nouns.places.geo.beach: A beach is the sandy edge where the land meets the water.
You build sandcastles, splash in the waves, and feel warm sand.
Try it like this: 'Let's go to the beach!'
nouns.places.geo.lake: A lake is a big pool of water with land all around it.
You can swim, fish, or ride a boat on a calm lake.
Try it like this: 'We swam in the lake.'
nouns.places.geo.forest: A forest is a big place full of many trees growing close together.
It's shady and green, and animals like deer and birds live there.
Try it like this: 'We walked in the forest.'
nouns.money.money: Money is what people use to pay for things they want.
It comes as paper bills and metal coins you trade at stores.
Try it like this: 'I need money to buy it.'
nouns.money.dollar: A dollar is a piece of paper money worth one hundred pennies.
You hand dollars to the cashier to pay for things.
Try it like this: 'That costs one dollar.'
nouns.money.coin: A coin is a small, round piece of metal money.
Pennies, nickels, dimes, and quarters are all coins.
Try it like this: 'I have a shiny coin.'
nouns.money.wallet: A wallet is a small folding pouch that holds money and cards.
You keep your dollars and cards tucked safely inside.
Try it like this: 'It's in my wallet.'
nouns.money.receipt: A receipt is a little paper that shows what you bought.
The cashier hands it to you after you pay.
Try it like this: 'Here's the receipt.'
nouns.money.list: A list is a written line of things you want to remember.
At the store you check off each thing you need to get.
Try it like this: 'It's on the list.'
nouns.money.cart: A cart is a basket on wheels you push around a store.
You put the things you're buying inside as you go.
Try it like this: 'Put it in the cart.'
nouns.money.change: Change is the money you get back when you pay with extra.
If something costs less than your dollar, you get coins back.
Try it like this: 'Here's your change.'
